- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals or daily/weekly wall-clock schedules; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

lelele，现在可以把结论往前推一大步了，我前面那条“首页手势把 switch 吃掉了”的怀疑，需要修正。

你这组输出已经说明了 3 件事：

- 触摸真实落点就是 `UISwitch` 内部视图：`_UILiquidLensView -> UISwitchModernVisualElement -> UISwitch -> WBHomeVappFeatureView`
- 首页这个 `tap` 手势在 [WBHomeViewController.m#L1157](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1157) 返回了 `NO`
- 所以“遮挡”和“首页 tap 手势拦截”这两条，基本都可以先排除

也就是说，问题已经缩到 `UISwitch` 自己这条链路上了。现在最值得查的是两件事：

- 这个 `UISwitch` 有没有被禁用
- 这个 `UISwitch` 还在不在给 `WBHomeVappFeatureView` 发 `ValueChanged`

你现在继续在 LLDB 里直接查这个开关本体，复制下面这几句就行：

```lldb
po (UISwitch *)[[[touch view] superview] superview]
po [(UISwitch *)[[[touch view] superview] superview] isEnabled]
po [(UISwitch *)[[[touch view] superview] superview] isUserInteractionEnabled]
po [(UISwitch *)[[[touch view] superview] superview] isOn]
po [(UISwitch *)[[[touch view] superview] superview] allTargets]
po [(UISwitch *)[[[touch view] superview] superview] actionsForTarget:(id)0x10a3de000 forControlEvent:UIControlEventValueChanged]
```

这里 `0x10a3de000` 就是你刚刚打出来的那个 `WBHomeVappFeatureView` 地址。

你看结果时就按这个标准判断：

- `isEnabled = 0`
  这就很像根因了。触摸能打到 switch，但控件本身不工作，所以 `switchValueChanged:` 不会进。
- `allTargets` 为空，或者 `actionsForTarget...` 返回 `nil`
  说明这个显示出来的开关没有绑上 `switchValueChanged:`，要回头查 view 重建或 target 丢失。
- `isEnabled = 1`，`allTargets` 里也有 `WBHomeVappFeatureView`，`actions` 也有 `switchValueChanged:`
  那就说明 target/action 都没问题。下一步就该看 `UISwitch` 有没有真的发 action。

如果走到第三种情况，再补一刀最直接：

1. 加一个符号断点：`-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]`
2. 再点一次这个 switch

判断就很干脆：

- 能断住，且 `self` 是 `UISwitch`
  说明 action 发出来了，后面再查为什么没进到 [WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/View/WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174)
- 根本断不住
  说明 `UISwitch` 这次压根没发 `ValueChanged`

你先把这 6 句 LLDB 输出贴我，我就能继续帮你往下收。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele

- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

      |                                                                                                    ^

lelele，现在信息已经很关键了，先把已经排除的说清楚：

- 你点到的确实是 `UISwitch` 本体，不是遮挡
- `UISwitch` 是可用的：`isEnabled = YES`，`isUserInteractionEnabled = YES`
- target 也还在：`allTargets` 里有 `WBHomeVappFeatureView`

所以现在基本不是“点不到”“被盖住”“没绑 target”这几类问题了。

接下来只差最后一步：确认这个 `UISwitch` 到底有没有发出 `ValueChanged`。

你先做这两个动作，还是不用改代码。

1. 先看 action 还在不在  
这句直接输，别用那个宏常量了，用数字最稳：

```lldb
po [(UISwitch *)[[[touch view] superview] superview] actionsForTarget:(id)0x10a3de000 forControlEvent:4096]
```

如果返回里有 `switchValueChanged:`，说明 action 也没丢。

2. 加两个符号断点再点一次  
在 Xcode 里加：

```text
-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]
-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]
```

然后再点一次右侧 switch。

看结果怎么分：

- `switchValueChanged:` 能断住  
  说明方法其实进了，只是你原来 176 行那个源码断点不准，偏向断点映射/编译产物问题。

- `sendAction:to:forEvent:` 能断住，但 `switchValueChanged:` 不断  
  说明控件发 action 了，但不是发给这个方法。停住时输：
  ```lldb
  po self
  po target
  po NSStringFromSelector(action)
  ```

- `sendAction:to:forEvent:` 都断不住  
  这就说明触摸虽然到了 `UISwitch`，但它最终没发 `ValueChanged`。这时候我会让你回头单查那个 `UILongPressGestureRecognizer`，因为 tap 已经排除了，剩下最可疑的就是别的手势在控件确认点击前把这次交互打断了。

你先把这 3 个结果贴我就行：
- `actionsForTarget` 的输出
- `sendAction:to:forEvent:` 有没有断住
- 如果断住了，`target` 和 `action` 是什么

有这一步，基本就能把根因钉死。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals or daily/weekly wall-clock schedules; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

lelele，`1` 这条已经很说明问题了：

- `UISwitch` 能响应
- target 在
- `ValueChanged` 对应的 action 也在，确实就是 `switchValueChanged:`

所以现在别盯 [WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/View/WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174) 那个源码断点了。下一步要用“符号断点”，因为 `sendAction` 不在你项目源码里，它是 UIKit 的方法，你在工程里找不到正常。

你这样做最稳：

1. 先把原来 176/177 的源码断点留着也行，但别只信它  
2. 在 Xcode 里加两个“Symbolic Breakpoint”
   - `-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]`
   - `-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]`

具体位置：

1. 打开 Xcode 左侧断点面板，快捷键 `cmd + 8`
2. 左下角点 `+`
3. 选 `Symbolic Breakpoint...`
4. 分别填上面两个名字
5. 再点一次那个 switch

`sendAction` 不需要找文件，它本来就不是你项目里的 `.m`。

如果你想直接用 LLDB，也可以在控制台输：

```lldb
breakpoint set --name "-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]"
breakpoint set --name "-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]"
```

然后你点一次 switch，看哪一个先停。

停住以后这样看：

- 如果停在 `-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]`，立刻输：
```lldb
po self
po target
po NSStringFromSelector(action)
```

你要看的是不是这种结果：

- `self` 是 `UISwitch`
- `target` 是 `WBHomeVappFeatureView`
- `action` 是 `switchValueChanged:`

如果是，那就说明事件已经发出来了。接下来如果 `-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]` 还不断，八成就是你原来那个源码断点不准，不是逻辑没走。

你现在先做这一轮就够了，把这 3 个输出贴我：

- `sendAction` 有没有停住
- `po self`
- `po target`
- `po NSStringFromSelector(action)`

我接着帮你往下钉，lelele。

所以现在别盯 [WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/View/WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174) 那个源码断点了。下一步要用“符号断点”，因为 `sendAction` 不在你项目源码里，它是 UIKit 的方法，你在工程里找不到正常。

你这样做最稳：

1. 先把原来 176/177 的源码断点留着也行，但别只信它  
2. 在 Xcode 里加两个“Symbolic Breakpoint”
   - `-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]`
   - `-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]`

breakpoint set --name "-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]"
breakpoint set --name "-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]"

然后你点一次 switch，看哪一个先停。

# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals or daily/weekly wall-clock schedules; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: 奥不对，在1157断住我继续之后又在1157断了一次，然后再继续就断在1161了

payload.message: 奥不对，在1157断住我继续之后又在1157断了一次，然后再继续就断在1161了

lelele，这个信息很关键，我前面那个“手势已经排除”的判断要收回来一半。

因为现在不是“只走了一次 `1157`”，而是：

- 先走了 `1157`
- 又走了 `1157`
- 最后还走到了 `1161`

这说明 `shouldReceiveTouch:` 对这次交互不是只判断了一次，而且最终确实有一次被放行了。只要有一次走到 [WBHomeViewController.m#L1161](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1161)，就说明仍然有某个手势在接这次 switch 的触摸。这个方向又重新变可疑了。

你现在别看 `1157` 那两次了，盯住“断在 `1161` 的那一次”，就在那一刻输这几句：

```lldb
po gestureRecognizer
po NSStringFromClass((Class)[gestureRecognizer class])
po [gestureRecognizer view]
po touch.view
po NSStringFromClass((Class)[[touch view] class])
```

如果还有余力，再补两句：

```lldb
po [[touch view] superview]
po NSStringFromClass((Class)[[[touch view] superview] class])
```

你现在重点只看一个结论：

- 如果 `1161` 那次的 `gestureRecognizer` 是 `UILongPressGestureRecognizer`
  那基本就快坐实了。首页的 `longpress` 还在吃 switch 这块触摸，之前的 `tap` 虽然有时被挡掉，但 `longpress` 最终还是放进来了。

- 如果 `1161` 那次是 `UITapGestureRecognizer`
  那也说明首页 tap 在某个时机还是接到了这次交互，只是要再看那一刻的 `touch.view` 是不是已经变了。

所以你现在不用再纠结 `switch` 自己绑没绑 action 了。  
`UISwitch` 本体、target、action 你都已经验过没问题了。现在最像根因的是：首页这两个手势里，至少有一个在某次判断里没有把 switch 触摸排干净。

你把“断在 `1161` 那次”的这 5 个 `po` 结果贴我，我就能直接告诉你是不是 `longpress`，以及后面的最小改法应该怎么收。

Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals or daily/weekly wall-clock schedules; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.

lelele，这次输出已经很像根因了。

关键不是 `1157` 那两次，而是你停在 [WBHomeViewController.m#L1161](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1161) 的那一次里：

- `gestureRecognizer` 是首页挂在 `collectionView` 上的 `UITapGestureRecognizer`
- 这次的 `touch.view` 已经不是 `UISwitch` 那条链了，而是 cell 里的一个普通 `UIView`
- 所以这次判断没有走到 `WBHomeVappFeatureView`，最后返回了 `YES`

这说明当前 [WBHomeViewController.m#L1153](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1153) 这套“沿着 `touch.view.superview` 找 `WBHomeVappFeatureView`”的过滤条件不稳定。同一次 switch 点击里，有的阶段它能识别成 feature 区，有的阶段又识别不出来，首页 tap 手势还是会混进来。

你刚才问的“2 怎么做”，这里我给你最直接的两个验证办法，不改代码。

1. 最硬的验证：临时关掉首页这两个手势，再点 switch  
在断住时直接输：

```lldb
po [self.collectionView gestureRecognizers]
```

先把 `tap` 和 `longpress` 的地址找出来，然后关掉它们：

```lldb
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x你的tap地址 setEnabled:NO]
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x你的longpress地址 setEnabled:NO]
```

然后 `continue`，再去点右侧 switch。

判断结果很直接：

- 如果这时候 switch 有反应，或者能断进 [WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/View/WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174)，那就基本坐实：首页手势就是根因。
- 如果关掉后还是没反应，再往 `UISwitch` 自己那条链继续查。

2. 你刚才问的符号断点，不是在源码里找  
可以直接在 LLDB 里下：

```lldb
breakpoint set --name "-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]"
breakpoint set --name "-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]"
```

它们不用在工程里有源码文件，也能断。

我建议你先走第 1 个，最快，也最像“真刀真枪”的排除法。你把 `gestureRecognizers` 那个输出贴我，我帮你把该关的两个地址直接指出来。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals or daily/weekly wall-clock schedules; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

lelele，找到了，首页自己加的两个手势就是这两个：

- `tap`：`0x15e99cb40`，对应 id `137`
- `longpress`：`0x13ad89a00`，对应 id `138`

而且你前面断在 `1161` 的那次，已经证明 `tap` 至少有一次被放进来了。所以现在最省事的验证就是先关 `tap`，再点一次 `switch`。

你直接在 LLDB 里输：

```lldb
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x15e99cb40 setEnabled:NO]
breakpoint set --name "-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]"
breakpoint set --name "-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]"
continue
```

然后再点右侧 `switch`，看结果：

- 如果这次能断进 `switchValueChanged:`，或者开关直接有反应，那基本就坐实了：首页这个 `tap` 手势在抢事件。
- 如果还不行，再把 `longpress` 也关掉再试一次：

```lldb
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x13ad89a00 setEnabled:NO]
continue
```

测完别忘了恢复：

```lldb
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x15e99cb40 setEnabled:YES]
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x13ad89a00 setEnabled:YES]
```

我现在的判断是：根因大概率就是 [WBHomeViewController.m#L1153](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1153) 这段过滤条件不稳，靠 `touch.view` 的父链判断 `WBHomeVappFeatureView`，同一次 switch 交互里有时识别得到，有时识别不到，所以首页 `tap` 还是会混进来。

你先做这一轮验证，把“关掉 tap 后有没有恢复”告诉我。只要这一步成立，我就能把后面的最小改法给你列清楚。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals or daily/weekly wall-clock schedules; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

你现在别继续乱点，直接在 LLDB 里输这几个：

```lldb
bt
po self
po target
po NSStringFromSelector(action)
```

你要看的结果是：

- `self` 是不是 `UISwitch`
- `target` 是不是 `WBHomeVappFeatureView`
- `action` 是不是 `switchValueChanged:`

如果这三个都对，就说明事件已经发出来了，链路其实是通的；这时你再输一次：

```lldb
continue
```

看会不会接着断进 `-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]`。

你把这 4 条输出贴我，我帮你直接下结论，lelele。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals or daily/weekly wall-clock schedules; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: 我已经过了，但没有断进-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]

payload.message: 我已经过了，但没有断进-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]

lelele，现在这一步还不能直接下结论。

因为 `-[UIControl sendAction:to:forEvent:]` 是所有 `UIControl` 共用入口。你这次直接 `continue` 过去了，但没看 `self / target / action`，所以那个断点不一定就是这颗 `UISwitch` 发出来的。

现在最干净的验证法，是先把首页自己加的两个手势都关掉，只留 `switchValueChanged:` 断点，再点一次。这样最省时间。

先在 LLDB 里输：

```lldb
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x15e99cb40 setEnabled:NO]
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x13ad89a00 setEnabled:NO]
breakpoint delete
breakpoint set --name "-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]"
continue
```

然后再点右侧 `switch`。

判断就两种：

- 能断进 `-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]`
  说明根因就是首页这两个手势，至少有一个在干扰 switch。对应代码就在 [WBHomeViewController.m#L187](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L187) 和 [WBHomeViewController.m#L193](\/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L193)。

- 还是断不进
  那就说明问题不在这两个手势，我们再回头精确查 `sendAction`，但那时要在断住的瞬间看：
  ```lldb
  po self
  po target
  po NSStringFromSelector(action)
  ```

测完以后记得恢复手势：

```lldb
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x15e99cb40 setEnabled:YES]
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x13ad89a00 setEnabled:YES]
```

你先跑这一轮，把“关掉两个手势后，能不能断进 `switchValueChanged:`”告诉我，我就能把结论收死。

- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

content.text: 不光vapp下载，downloading res时也点不了

payload.message: 不光vapp下载，downloading res时也点不了

lelele，这个补充很关键，方向反而更清楚了。

这说明问题大概率不是某个具体业务分支，比如“一键启动在 vapp 下载中被禁用”这种。因为你现在看到的是两种不同状态都点不了：

1. `WBVappStatusDownloading`
2. `WBVappStatusResourceDownloading`

这两种状态后面的业务处理完全不是一套，但它们在首页走的是同一层 UI 链路：
- 同一个 feature 视图入口：[WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/View/WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174)
- 同一个首页手势入口：[WBHomeViewController.m#L1153](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1153)

再结合你前面已经确认的几件事：

- 触摸确实打到了 `UISwitch`
- `UISwitch` 是 `enabled = YES`
- target 和 action 都还在
- 但 `shouldReceiveTouch:` 同一次交互里既走过 `1157`，又走过 `1161`

我现在更偏向这个结论：

首页挂在 `collectionView` 上的 `tap/longpress` 手势，对 `switch` 这块区域的过滤不稳定。不是每次都能把 feature 区排干净，所以同一类问题会同时出现在“下载中”和“资源下载中”。

下一步别再分状态看了，直接做这个最硬的验证，5 分钟内能定性：

```lldb
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x15e99cb40 setEnabled:NO]
expr [(UIGestureRecognizer *)0x13ad89a00 setEnabled:NO]
breakpoint delete
breakpoint set --name "-[WBHomeVappFeatureView switchValueChanged:]"
continue
```

然后再点一次右侧 switch。

判断标准就一个：

- 如果这次能断进 `switchValueChanged:`，根因就基本坐实是首页这两个手势
- 如果还进不去，那我再带你查下一层，但现在从证据上看，前一种概率更大

如果这个验证成立，我建议的改法会是最小改动这套，不先动业务层：

1. 改 [WBHomeViewController.m#L1153](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1153) 的过滤逻辑，不再只认 `WBHomeVappFeatureView`，而是把 `UIControl/UISwitch` 整条父链都排除。
2. 给首页那个 `longpress` 也补 `cancelsTouchesInView = NO`。
3. 只改首页手势，不碰 `WBHomeVappFeatureView` 和 `WBBenefitManager`。

你先跑这一轮验证，把结果告诉我。我再把最终改动点收成一版给你确认。

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
# 全局协作约定（系统级）

1. 我提出的所有问题，都不要直接改代码；必须先给出方案/改动点，并与我确认后再开始修改。
2. 对我提供的设计图：忽略其中的虚线边框（不作为需要实现的 UI/布局边界）。
3. 默认使用 `shuorenhua` 的表达规则回答：优先说人话、去模板感、自然一点，减少 AI 套话和空洞连接词；但不能改变事实、结论、数据、术语和代码语义。除非我明确要求保留正式/模板化表达，否则都按这个规则执行。
const additionalDeveloperPrompt = `# 必须遵守的行为规范\n
* 必须使用中文回答\n
* 在遇到需要探索源码的任务时，拆分出关键词，进行grep搜索，一次性读取所有相关文件进行分析，读文件时如果文件代码没有超过600行，不要分段读取，一次全部读入所有内容，必须在最少的交互会话次数内完成工作\n
* 编辑文件时，如果涉及到整个文件的过多次修改。不要使用Edit工具多次修改，而必须使用Write工具一次性生成文件。生成代码时，最大限度保持现有代码，不要随意格式化代码，只修改或增加必要的部分\n
* 模仿claude的风格回复用户\n
* 生成代码时，可读性一定要好，便于人类理解，但不需要很多注释。在保证逻辑清晰，正确的基础上要做到代码精炼，用最少的代码做最多的事情，但是不能破坏可读性。\n`
* 每次回复都喊我lelele
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.
- If workspace dependencies are missing, call `install_workspace_dependencies` first.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring tasks/automations
- Automations are stored as TOML in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml (not in SQLite). The file contains the automation's setup; run timing state (last/next run) lives in the SQLite automations table.

#### When to use the automation tool
- Only use the `automation_update` tool when the user explicitly asks for automation, a recurring run, or a repeated task.
- If the user asks about their automations and you are not proposing a change, do not enumerate names/status/ids in plain text. Fetch/list automations first and call `automation_update` with mode="view" for those ids; never invent ids.
- Never return raw RRULE strings in user-facing responses. If the user asks about their automations, respond using `automation_update` (e.g., with an "Open" button if you're not making changes).

#### Tool arguments
- Modes: view, suggested_update, suggested_create. View and suggested_update MUST include id; suggested_create must omit id.
- For view calls, id is required and other fields are optional (the UI can load details).
- For suggested_update/suggested_create, include name, prompt, rrule, kind, and status.
- For cron automations, include cwds, executionEnvironment, model, and reasoningEffort. cwds can be a JSON array or comma-separated string.
- Always come up with a short name for the automation. If the user does not give one, propose a short name and confirm.
- Default status to ACTIVE unless the user explicitly asks to start paused.
- Always interpret and schedule times in the user's locale time zone.

#### Prompting guidance
- Ask in plain language what it should do, when it should run, and which workspaces or thread it should use (if any), then map those answers into the tool call arguments.
- The automation prompt should describe only the task itself. Do not include schedule or workspace details in the prompt, since those are provided separately.
- Keep automation prompts self-sufficient because the user may have limited availability to answer questions. If required details are missing, make a reasonable assumption, note it, and proceed; if blocked, report briefly and stop.
- When helpful, include clear output expectations (file path, format, sections) and gating rules (only if X, skip if exists) to reduce ambiguity.
- Automations should always open an inbox item.
- Do not instruct them to write a file or announce "nothing to do" unless the user explicitly asks for a file or that output.
- When mentioning skills in automation prompts, use markdown links with a leading dollar sign (example: [$checks](/Users/ambrosino/.codex/skills/checks/SKILL.md)).

#### Cron scheduling constraints
- These RRULE limitations apply to cron automations (to match the current UI): only hourly interval schedules (FREQ=HOURLY with INTERVAL hours, optional BYDAY) and weekly schedules (FREQ=WEEKLY with BYDAY plus BYHOUR/BYMINUTE). Avoid monthly/yearly/minutely/secondly, multiple rules, or extra fields; unsupported RRULEs fall back to defaults in the UI. Heartbeat automations attached to a thread can also use minute-based intervals or daily/weekly wall-clock schedules; see the thread automation guidance below.

#### Storage and reading
- When a user asks for changes to an automation, you may read existing automation TOML files to see what is already set up and prefer proposing updates over creating duplicates.
- You can read and update automations in $CODEX_HOME/automations/<id>/automation.toml and memory.md only when the user explicitly asks you to modify automations.
- Otherwise, do not change automation files or schedules.
- Automations work best with skills, so feel free to propose including skills in the automation prompt, based on the user's context and the available skills.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- Branch prefix: `codex/`. Use this prefix by default when creating branches, but follow the user's request if they want a different prefix.
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

lelele，`expr` 那两个 `EXC_BAD_ACCESS` 可以先忽略，更多像是 LLDB 直接拿裸地址发消息时炸了，不影响我们前面已经拿到的证据。

我现在的判断已经比较稳了：问题在首页 `collectionView` 上那两个手势，不在 `switch` 本身，也不在下载中的业务分支。

依据是这几条已经对上了：

- 你点到的确实是 `UISwitch`，父链也能回到 `WBHomeVappFeatureView`
- `UISwitch` 是可交互的，target/action 也都在，`switchValueChanged:` 没丢
- 但同一次点击里，`shouldReceiveTouch:` 先后既走过 [WBHomeViewController.m#L1157](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1157)，又最终走到 [WBHomeViewController.m#L1161](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1161)
- 走到 `1161` 那次，`touch.view` 已经变成 cell 里的普通 `UIView`，不是 `UISwitch` 那条链了，所以首页 `tap` 被放进来了

这就说明现在这段过滤逻辑不稳：

- [WBHomeViewController.m#L1153](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1153)

它现在是“只要顺着 `touch.view.superview` 能找到 `WBHomeVappFeatureView` 就拦掉”，但实际一次 `switch` 交互里，`touch.view` 并不总是稳定落在同一条父链上，所以会漏。

我建议的改法不是继续补丁式判断 `touch.view`，而是直接把首页手势改成白名单，只允许它处理自己真正该处理的区域：

1. 在 [WBHomeViewController.m#L1153](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/Main/WBHomeViewController.m#L1153) 改成按触点位置判断。
2. 只有点在 `vappIconImageView` 或“加号 cell”区域时，首页 `tap/longpress` 才返回 `YES`。
3. 点在右侧 feature 区，包括 `UISwitch`、隐藏/编辑按钮、更多功能区域，统一返回 `NO`。
4. 顺手给首页的 `longpress` 也补上 `cancelsTouchesInView = NO`，做双保险。
5. 不改 [WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174](/Users/liuyifu/Documents/Bface/weihe/WeBox/WeBox/UI/Pages/Home/Controller/View/WBHomeVappFeatureView.m#L174) 和后面的 `WBBenefitManager` 业务逻辑。

这样改的好处是：

- 逻辑更正，不再依赖不稳定的 `touch.view` 父链
- 能同时覆盖你说的两种状态：`downloading` 和 `downloading res`
- 影响面只在首页手势层，风险最小

你如果确认，我就按这个最小方案改。
